calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-27-2023 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Harry L Jacobs; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:29 am and concluding at 9:39 am.

Plaintiff Bank of America N.A is represented by Jonathan Vaknin, Esq. via zoom video conference.
Defendant Ahmad Fawad is represented by Theresia Sandhu, Esq. specially appearing.

Plaintiff's Ex Parte Application for Waiver of Witness Requirement and Permission to Proceed by Declaration Pursuant to San Francisco County Superior Court Local Rule 6.6(D)(7)(b) was filed on 9/21/2023.

Declaration of Plaintiff Richard Nichols in lieu of personal testimony is ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE.

Plaintiff's counsel presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Bank of America N.A, and against Defendant Ahmad Fawad, as follows:

Damages: $12,945.95
Costs: $503.50

Total Judgment: $13,449.45
Proposed judgment signed and filed. (D505)

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-20-2023 Continued To Oct-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar May-17-2023 Continued To Sep-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for short cause trial.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:24:26 a.m. and concluding at 09:27:55 a.m.

Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A. represented by William Bellor-Yeh, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Ahmad Fawad not appearing.

Plaintiff's oral 170.6 Challenge against Judge Michelle Tong is GRANTED.
Court orders matter continued to Sep-20-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 to be heard by a visiting judge.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To May-17-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being continued from Jan-30-2023 for commencement of short cause trial/prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:07:54 a.m. and concluding at 11:13:15 a.m.

Plaintiff Bank of America , N. A. represented by William Bellor-yeh, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Ahmad Fawad not appearing.

Plaintiff has no witnesses.
Order Deeimng Requests for Admissions Admitted filed Mar-28-2022.

Court determines the following:

1. Proposed Judgment has not been submitted
2. DOES 1-5 have not been dismissed
3. Notice of Trial and Place of Trial and Trial Order sent only to Defendant's counsel, and not to individual Defendant. Defendant's counsel has not responded.

Counsel for Plaintiff shall submit proposed judgment and file request for dismissal of DOES 1-5.

Court orders matter continued to May-17-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for further short cause trial/prove-up hearing. Plaintiff to give notice to Defendant. Clerk to mail notice of hearing to all parties, including individual Defendant.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2022 Set For Trial On Jan-30-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Set For Case Management Conference On Oct-12-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Kenneth Tsang, Esq. specially appearing for the plaintiff. Plaintiff's counsel reported that the defendant did not comply with the terms of the settlement and requested that the case be returned to the trial calendar.
